cards.back: A string matching algorithm that preprocesses the pattern to reduce the number of comparisons needed during the matching process

cards.front: How does Horspool's Algorithm handle mismatched characters?
cards.back: By shifting the pattern to the right and adjusting the shift value based on the mismatched character

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Horspool's Algorithm?
cards.back: O(n + m), where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern

cards.front: What is the space complexity of Horspool's Algorithm?
cards.back: O(1), as it only uses a constant amount of space to store the shift values

cards.front: What is the purpose of the shift table in Horspool's Algorithm?
cards.back: To store the shift values for each character in the pattern, allowing for efficient shifting of the pattern during the matching process
